title: Nexus 9000 NX-OS Model-Driven Telemetry Streaming Health (gRPC Dial-Out, Sensor Groups, TLS, Heartbeats)
exclusions: **NDI anomaly-only** feeds (**UC-18.4.1**); **NDFC** template/**POAP** posture (**UC-18.4.2**); **PSIRT**/**EoX** lifecycle (**UC-18.4.3**); **BGP IPv4-AF**/**L2VPN-EVPN-AF**/**NVE** peers (**UC-18.4.5**); **CoPP MQC** drops (**UC-18.4.6**); **NDFC Golden Image**/**SUM**/**ISSU** (**UC-18.4.10**). This UC watches **telemetry transport**, **sensor subscriptions**, **TLS**, **Protobuf encoding**, **gRPC dial-out**, **heartbeat** cadence—not routing adjacency.
evidence: Saved search **`nxos_uc1844_mdt_health`** across **`index=cisco_dc`** (**`cisco:nxos:telemetry`**, **`cisco:nxos:gnmi`**, **`cisco:nxos:show:telemetry`**, **`cisco:nxos:syslog`**) plus Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_nxos_mdt_health.json`** wired with **`tok_switch`**, **`tok_sensor`**, **`tok_exporter`** drill tokens.
dataSources: `index=cisco_dc` / `index=cisco_network` merging **`sourcetype=cisco:nxos:telemetry`** dial-out **gRPC** payloads (**gpb-compact**, **kvgpb**, **json**) keyed by **`sensor_path`**, **`subscription_id`**, **`encoding`**, **`cadence_sec`**; **`sourcetype=cisco:nxos:gnmi`** collector-side **`Subscribe`** narratives; **`sourcetype=cisco:nxos:show:telemetry`** CLI snapshots referencing **`show telemetry transport`** (**TX_FAILED**, queue depth); **`sourcetype=cisco:nxos:syslog`** mnemonics **`TELEMETRY_INSTANCE_PROFILE-2-`** telemetry bootstrap faults; **`sourcetype=splunk_observability_otel`** / **`otel`** HECE showing OTel Collector **`gnmi`** receiver errors (**TLS**, decode); optional **`sourcetype=vmware:vsphere:syslog`** timestamp drift notices correlated—not causal—for **`grpc_tcp_keepalive_expiry`** hunts.
app: **Cisco DC Networking Application for Splunk** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)); **Splunk Add-on for Cisco IOS/NX-OS** ([Splunkbase **2846**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2846)); **Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector** (**gnmi** receiver → **HEC**); Heavy Forwarder **syslog** inputs toward **`cisco:nxos:syslog`**.
spl:
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=otel_metrics)
(sourcetype="cisco:nxos:telemetry" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:gnmi" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:show:telemetry" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="splunk_observability_otel")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval collector_plane="grpc_dial_out"
| eval uc_hint="nxos_mdt_uc1844"
| eval host_key=trim(lower(coalesce(host,switch_hostname,nexus_hostname,"")))
| eval sensor_key=trim(lower(coalesce(sensor_path,yang_sensor_path,gpb_path,"")))
| eval enc_txt=lower(trim(coalesce(encoding,gpb_encoding,telemetry_encoding,"")))
| eval cadence=tonumber(coalesce(cadence_sec,sample_interval,sensor_period,"0"))
| eval hb_sec=tonumber(coalesce(heartbeat_interval_sec,heartbeat_sec,"0"))
| eval suppress_flag=upper(trim(coalesce(suppress_redundant,suppress_duplicates,"")))
| eval tx_fail=tonumber(coalesce(tx_failed,telemetry_tx_failed,counters_tx_failed,"0"))
| eval queue_drop=tonumber(coalesce(queue_drop,telemetry_queue_drop,pipeline_drop,"0"))
| eval syslog_hit=match(_raw,"(?i)TELEMETRY_INSTANCE_PROFILE-2-")
| eval otel_err=match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(decode.*fail|protobuf|marshal|tls handshake|keepalive)")
| eval mdt_fault=case(
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(tls.*rotate|cert.*renew)"),"mdt_tls_cert_rotation_window",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(sensor.?subscription.*warm|subscription.?start)"),"sensor_subscription_warmup",
    cadence>0 AND cadence<=5 AND match(sensor_key,"(?i)cisco-nx-os-device"),"mdt_cadence_change",
    suppress_flag IN ("TRUE","YES","ON","1"),"suppress_redundant_toggle",
    hb_sec>0 AND hb_sec!=60,"mdt_heartbeat_change",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(keepalive.*expir|tcp keepalive)"),"grpc_tcp_keepalive_expiry",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(yang.?path.?migrat|sensor.?path.?chang)"),"yang_path_migration_window",
    match(enc_txt,"(?i)^(json|compact)$") AND match(sensor_key,"(?i)cisco-nx-os-device"),"mdt_encoding_change",
    sourcetype="cisco:nxos:show:telemetry" AND tx_fail>0,"telemetry_tx_failed",
    sourcetype="cisco:nxos:telemetry" AND queue_drop>10,"telemetry_queue_overflow",
    syslog_hit,"telemetry_syslog_bootstrap",
    otel_err,"otel_decode_or_tls_fault",
    null())
| where isnotnull(mdt_fault)
| bin _time span=5m
| stats latest(enc_txt) as encoding_latest,
        latest(cadence) as cadence_latest,
        latest(hb_sec) as heartbeat_latest,
        latest(tx_fail) as tx_fail_latest,
        latest(queue_drop) as queue_drop_latest,
        dc(sensor_key) as sensor_paths_seen,
        latest(host_key) as host_key_latest,
        count as evt_cnt
    by _time host_key mdt_fault
| eval pair_story=host_key." | ".mdt_fault
| eval exporter_health=if(tx_fail_latest>0 OR queue_drop_latest>0,"degraded","nominal")
| eval cadence_watch=if(cadence_latest>0 AND cadence_latest<10,"cadence_hot","cadence_ok")
| sort - evt_cnt
| head 400
| table _time host_key mdt_fault encoding_latest cadence_latest heartbeat_latest tx_fail_latest queue_drop_latest sensor_paths_seen evt_cnt pair_story exporter_health cadence_watch uc_hint
description: Splunk monitors **Cisco Nexus 9000** **NX-OS** **model-driven telemetry** (**MDT**) **gRPC dial-out** transports (**TCP 9339**, **50051** variants), **sensor-group** **YANG** paths rooted at **`Cisco-NX-OS-device`**, **subscription** knobs (**cadence**, **depth**, **`suppress-redundant`**, **`heartbeat-interval`**), **encoding** transitions (**gpb-compact**, **json**, **compact**), **TLS** lifecycle hooks, OTel Collector **Protobuf** decode health, **`show telemetry transport`** counters (**TX_FAILED**, queue spills), CLI **`telemetry`** snapshots, syslog **`TELEMETRY_INSTANCE_PROFILE-2-`** bootstrap arcs, and optional **VMware** clock notices—surfacing stalled exporters before BGP dashboards lose parity.
value: **Dashboard gaps** usually precede **`telemetry_tx_failed`** spikes—Splunk merges **`cisco:nxos:telemetry`**, **`cisco:nxos:show:telemetry`**, **`cisco:nxos:syslog`**, and OTel **`splunk_observability_otel`** errors so operators differentiate **`mdt_tls_cert_rotation_window`** episodes from **`sensor_subscription_warmup`** churn, **`mdt_cadence_change`** drills (**30s→5s**), **`suppress_redundant_toggle`** regressions, **`mdt_heartbeat_change`** edits, **`grpc_tcp_keepalive_expiry`** faults, **`yang_path_migration_window`** cutovers, and **`mdt_encoding_change`** migrations (**gpb→json**) ahead of silent KPI blanks.
implementation: **Quick win:** ingest **`cisco:nxos:show:telemetry`** scripted snapshots plus **`TELEMETRY_INSTANCE_PROFILE`** syslog patterns via Splunk Add-on Cisco IOS/NX-OS (**2846**)—stamp **`host`**—before layering OTel **`gnmi`** pipelines.
schema: CIM **Performance** overlays **`cadence_sec`**/**`heartbeat`** pacing while **Alerts** captures **`mdt_fault`** classifications analogous to vendor telemetry alarms.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Alerts** after **`FIELDALIAS`** maps **`mdt_fault`** strings into normalized **`signature`** fields keyed by **`host`**.

references.title: Cisco DC Networking Application for Splunk (Splunkbase 7777)
Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS Programmability Configuration Guide — telemetry
Splunk Observability OpenTelemetry Collector — gNMI receiver

knownFalsePositives:
**mdt_tls_cert_rotation_window** — Planned **`telemetry`** **`certificate`** rotations deliberately widen **`TLS`** handshake gaps—suppress **`telemetry_syslog_bootstrap`** bursts tied to **`CHG`** bundles referencing **`trustpoint`** rehearsals before accusing **`grpc`** outages.

**sensor_subscription_warmup** — Fresh **`sensor-group`** onboarding (**`Cisco-NX-OS-device`** subtrees) emits **`sensor_subscription_warmup`** gaps lasting minutes—monitor **`yang_path_migration_window`** transcripts referencing **`CHG`** identifiers rather than steady-state exporters alone.

**mdt_cadence_change** — Operators deliberately tighten **`cadence_sec`** (**30→5**) for hotspots—**`mdt_cadence_change`** flags mimic pathology unless dashboards annotate observability **`CHG`** stamps beside **`suppress_redundant_toggle`** contexts.

**suppress_redundant_toggle** — Flip **`suppress-redundant`** during troubleshooting floods Splunk with **`telemetry_queue_overflow`** proxies lacking dataplane faults—pair toggles with **`pipeline`** **`heap`** metrics from OTel **`splunk_observability_otel`** feeds.

**mdt_heartbeat_change** — **`heartbeat-interval`** edits (**60→30**) reorder exporter pacing yet dataplane counters remain calm—tie pager macros to **`CHG`** approvals referencing **`grpc`** **`subscription`** edits.

**grpc_tcp_keepalive_expiry** — WAN TCP keepalive variance triggers benign **`grpc_tcp_keepalive_expiry`** bursts—cross-check ICMP probes plus **`vmware:vsphere:syslog`** drift notices before cabling escalation.

**yang_path_migration_window** — Vendor **`YANG`** revisions relocate **`DbgIfHCIn`** sensors—temporary **`yang_path_migration_window`** alarms coincide with **`pipeline`** **`reload`** narratives lacking congestion signatures.

**mdt_encoding_change** — Migration **`gpb-compact`**→**`json`** stresses CPUs momentarily—**`mdt_encoding_change`** bursts resemble outages unless **`CHG`** cites telemetry modernization windows documented beside **`encoding_latest`** dashboards.
detailedImplementation:
**Prerequisites**
• Install Splunk_TA_cisco-ios (`2846`) wherever **`cisco:nxos:syslog`** terminates—preserve **`TELEMETRY_INSTANCE_PROFILE-2-`** mnemonics.
• Deploy Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector with `gnmi` receiver exporting toward HEC (`8088`)—normalize **`splunk_observability_otel`** sourcetype spelling consistent with Observability docs.
• Heavy Forwarders schedule scripted NX-API or SSH collectors emitting **`show telemetry transport`** snapshots (**`cisco:nxos:show:telemetry`**) every five minutes balancing CPU budgets.
• Maintain RBAC credentials for Nexus CLI automation isolated from interactive operator accounts.

**Configure ingestion**
(1) Enable NX-OS dial-out telemetry profiles targeting collector VIPs (`TCP 9339`/`50051`) with TLS trustpoints aligned to organizational PKI—stamp `sensor_path`, `cadence_sec`, `encoding`, **`suppress_redundant`**, **`heartbeat_interval`**.
(2) Route syslog (**`facility local7`**) carrying telemetry bootstrap faults without stripping timestamps—critical when correlating **`mdt_tls_cert_rotation_window`** bursts with OTel TLS traces.
(3) OTel Collector pipelines must expose decode errors (`Protobuf`, `grpc`) as structured logs—not silently drop counters—landing **`splunk_observability_otel`**.
(4) Optional VMware vSphere syslog notices (**`vmware:vsphere:syslog`**) tag hypervisor clock drift unrelated yet helpful when diagnosing **`grpc_tcp_keepalive_expiry`** alongside ESXi hosts feeding telemetry taps.

**Search design**
Paste bundled `spl` into **`macros/nxos_mdt_health_core`** once index aliases (`cisco_dc`, **`cisco_network`**, **`otel_metrics`**) stabilize.
Treat **`telemetry_tx_failed`** paired with **`queue_drop_latest`** as Sev-2 evidence absent CHG suppressions referencing **`yang_path_migration_window`**.
Throttle **`sensor_subscription_warmup`** alerts until sequential buckets prove sustained backlog—not sixty-second glitch bursts tied to exporter restart ordering.
Annotate dashboards with **`encoding_latest`** facets distinguishing **`mdt_encoding_change`** migrations (`gpb-compact`→`json`) from genuine stalls.

**Validate**
• Compare Splunk `pair_story` tuples against Nexus CLI **`show telemetry transport`** counters (`TX_FAILED`) per `host_key`.
• Capture **`show telemetry exporter`** transcripts proving TLS profiles whenever **`mdt_tls_cert_rotation_window`** arcs spike alongside syslog banners.
• Correlate OTel **`splunk_observability_otel`** WARN traces referencing decode failures versus NX-OS exporter revisions documented inside Cisco release notes.
• Exercise gnmi Subscribe CLI parity (`grpcurl`) during tabletop drills validating Splunk `_time` skew stays inside cadence tolerance bands.

**Operational runbook**
• Run Sev-3 paging when **`telemetry_queue_overflow`** persists eight consecutive buckets without CHG bundles referencing **`suppress_redundant_toggle`** rehearsals.
• Escalate Sev-2 whenever **`otel_decode_or_tls_fault`** aligns with NX-OS ISSU freezes documented inside maintenance portals.
• Weekly trending — summarize `evt_cnt` histograms per `mdt_fault` feeding quarterly modernization decks contrasting JSON versus GPB encoding CPU footprints (**`mdt_encoding_change`**).
• Troubleshooting quick hits — stale collectors (`401`) widen gaps—inspect Heavy Forwarder `splunkd.log`; duplicate `sensor_path` merges across VRF contexts require dedupe keys anchored on `host_key` plus **`subscription_id`**.

**Governance cadence**
Quarterly audits reconcile Splunk anomaly timelines against Cisco-published telemetry sizing envelopes—justify supervisor uplifts ahead of exporter starvation unrelated to cabling faults.

**Evidence hygiene**
Archive sanitized Splunk exports beside NX-CLI screenshot bundles whenever auditors challenge telemetry completeness—dual evidence chains accelerate approvals versus spreadsheets lacking `_time` provenance.


**Bold glossary reinforcement**
• Reinforce **`mdt_tls_cert_rotation_window`** narratives beside `trustpoint` overlays anchored on `TLS` telemetry cues.
• Reinforce **`sensor_subscription_warmup`** narratives beside **`Cisco-NX-OS-device`** overlays anchored on **`sensor_group`** telemetry cues.
• Reinforce **`mdt_cadence_change`** narratives beside `cadence_sec` overlays anchored on **`subscription`** telemetry cues.
• Reinforce **`suppress_redundant_toggle`** narratives beside **`suppress_duplicates`** overlays anchored on `pipeline` telemetry cues.
• Reinforce **`mdt_heartbeat_change`** narratives beside **`heartbeat_interval_sec`** overlays anchored on `grpc` telemetry cues.
• Reinforce **`grpc_tcp_keepalive_expiry`** narratives beside **`TCP keepalive`** overlays anchored on `WAN` telemetry cues.
• Reinforce **`yang_path_migration_window`** narratives beside `DbgIfHCIn` overlays anchored on `YANG` telemetry cues.
• Reinforce **`mdt_encoding_change`** narratives beside `gpb-compact` overlays anchored on `json` telemetry cues.

grandmaExplanation: We watch whether your switches keep streaming their minute-by-minute health numbers cleanly—pipes staying open and timers honest—before dashboards freeze while the cables themselves still carry traffic.
hardware: Cisco Nexus 9000 NX-OS standalone running model-driven telemetry (MDT) streaming via gRPC dial-out to OpenTelemetry Collector or Splunk HEC
